pi.summary: PI Controller — Core of the control loop
pi.text:
Controlled variable: grid power (positive = import, negative = export). Manipulated variable: AC output power of the inverter.

P-part: responds immediately to the current deviation (grid − offset).
I-part: accumulates deviations over time, eliminates steady-state error.
Anti-windup: integral clamped to ±1000.
Integral reset: on every zone transition.
Tolerance decay: −5 %/cycle when error ≤ dead band and |integral| > 10.
Zone 0: integral frozen, no PI call.

Error calculation depends on mode:
• Normal: raw_error = grid − offset
• AC Charge: raw_error = offset − grid (inverted)

Self-Adjusting Wait: After each output change the controller waits for the actual inverter output power instead of a fixed pause. Once actual_power reaches the target ±tolerance, the controller continues. The configured wait time acts as a maximum timeout.

Starting values: P = 0.5, I = 0. Increase P gradually until output starts oscillating, then step back. Only introduce I once P-control is stable.

zones.summary: SOC Zone Logic — behaviour based on battery state of charge
zones.text:
Zone 0 (surplus, optional): SOC ≥ export threshold AND PV surplus → output at hard limit. Discharge current 2 A. PI integral frozen.

Zone 1 (aggressive): SOC > zone-1 threshold → high discharge current, offset 1. Runs through until SOC ≤ zone-3 threshold — no yo-yo effect.

Zone 2 (conservative): zone-3 < SOC ≤ zone-1 → 0 A discharge current, dynamic output limit (max PV − reserve), offset 2.

Zone 3 (safety stop): SOC ≤ zone-3 threshold → output 0 W, mode disabled. AC charging remains possible.

surplus.text:
Highest priority — blocks tariff charging, discharge-lock and AC charging while Zone 0 is active.

Normal entry: SOC ≥ SOC threshold AND (PV > output + grid + PV hysteresis OR PV = 0 OR output = 0).
Forecast entry: forecast ≥ threshold AND PV > hard limit (no SOC gate).

Exit (without forecast only): SOC < (threshold − SOC hysteresis) OR PV ≤ output + grid − PV hysteresis (when PV ≠ 0 and output ≠ 0).
With active forecast: exit completely blocked — only Zone 3 (safety) ends surplus. Once the forecast sensor drops below the threshold, normal exit logic applies immediately.

Output is set to hard limit. Integral frozen (no PI call).

ac.summary: AC Charging — grid charges the battery
ac.text:
Entry (case G): SOC < target AND no surplus active AND no AC/tariff charge active AND mode ≠ '3' AND (grid + output) < −hysteresis.
Abort (case H): mode = '3' AND ac_charge_active AND no tariff charge AND (SOC ≥ target OR (grid ≥ offset + hysteresis AND output = 0 W)).

PI controller runs inverted — positive error → increase charge power.
at_max/at_min guards removed — case I handles the safety function.

Return: Zone 1 → timer toggle + mode '1'. Zone 2 → output 0 W + mode '0'. Integral = 0.

SOC protection (Zone 3) remains fully active.

Due to the Solakon ONE hardware ramp (~25 s from min to max), keep the P-factor low. The I-part does the actual control work.

Priority: AC charging does not start if surplus feed-in (Zone 0) is active. AC charging and tariff charging mutually block each other via the mode guard (mode ≠ '3').

tariff.summary: Tariff Arbitrage (Tibber, aWATTar …) — three price tiers
tariff.text:
Cheap (price < cheap threshold): tariff charging starts — fixed charge power until SOC target. No PI controller.
Medium (cheap ≤ price < expensive threshold): discharge-lock — Zone 1 and Zone 2 blocked (output 0 W, mode disabled). Once the price exceeds the expensive threshold, operation is automatically restored.
Expensive (price ≥ expensive threshold): normal SOC logic, no restriction.

Tariff charging and AC charging are independent modules that mutually block each other via the mode guard.

Priority: tariff charging and discharge-lock are blocked while surplus feed-in (Zone 0) is active.

Requires a price sensor with a numeric value in ct/kWh.

dynoff.summary: Dynamic Offset — automatically calculated from grid volatility
dynoff.text:
Calculates the zero-point offset from the standard deviation of grid power over the configured time window.

Formula: offset = clamp(min + max(0, (StdDev − noise) × factor), min, max)

Example (min=30, noise=15, factor=1.5):
• StdDev 5 W → 30 W (minimum)
• StdDev 30 W → 53 W
• StdDev 80 W → 128 W
• StdDev 160 W → 228 W
• StdDev 250 W+ → 250 W (maximum)

Each zone (Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone AC) can be enabled individually and overrides the static offset of that zone.

The StdDev sensor is calculated internally — no external statistics sensor required.

night.summary: Night Shutdown — disable Zone 2 when PV < PV reserve
night.text:
Affects Zone 2 only (case F): mode → disabled, output 0 W, integral reset.
Zone 1 and AC charging continue running at night.

Threshold: PV reserve from zone settings — no separate parameter.

Reactivation: once PV rises above the PV reserve again, case E applies (integral reset, timer toggle, mode → '1') — provided no tariff lock is active.

debug.summary: Debug — manual intervention in the control state
debug.text:
Reset integral: sets the I-part of the PI controller to 0.

Set zone manually: toggles the internal cycle_active flag and resets the integral.
• Activate Zone 1: cycle_active = true → aggressive discharge, full discharge current.
• Activate Zone 2: cycle_active = false → battery-friendly operation, 0 A discharge current, dynamic output limit.

All actions are logged under 'Last action' in the Status tab.

p_factor.d: Responds immediately to the current deviation (grid − offset). Higher = more aggressive. Too high: output oscillates permanently. Typical after tuning: 0.8–1.5

i_factor.d: Accumulates deviations over time, eliminates steady-state error. Anti-windup clamped to ±1000. Starting value: 0 — only increase once P-control is stable. Typical: 0.03–0.08

tolerance.d: Deviations within this range do not trigger a PI action. Instead the integral decay kicks in (−5 %/cycle when |integral| > 10).

wait_time.d: Compensates hardware ramp + sensor polling latency after an output change. Without self-adjust: fixed pause. With self-adjust: maximum timeout as safety net.

self_adjust_enabled.d: After each output change, waits for the actual inverter output power. Once actual_power reaches the target ±tolerance, the controller continues. The wait time acts as a maximum timeout.

self_adjust_tolerance.d: Deviation between actual_power and target in watts at which the target is considered reached and the wait ends. Typical: 2–5 W.

zone1_limit.d: SOC exceeds this value → Zone 1 starts. Zone 1 runs through until SOC ≤ Zone 3 threshold — no yo-yo effect. Also active at night.

zone3_limit.d: SOC drops below this value → Zone 3 (safety stop): output 0 W, mode disabled. Must be less than Zone 1 threshold. AC charging remains possible despite Zone 3.

pv_reserve.d: Zone-2 output limit: max(0, PV − reserve). Also the threshold for night shutdown: Zone 2 disabled when PV < reserve.

hard_limit.d: Absolute output power ceiling in Zone 0 and Zone 1. The at_max_limit guard only applies at this ceiling, not at the dynamic Zone-2 limit.

discharge_max.d: Discharge current in Zone 1. Zone 2 and AC charging automatically set 0 A. Surplus sets 2 A.

offset_1.d: Control target in Zone 1. Positive = controller maintains slight grid import as safety buffer. Negative = controller slightly exports. Overridden when dynamic offset for Zone 1 is active.

offset_2.d: Control target in Zone 2. Recommended: slightly larger than Zone-1 offset (stronger import buffer when battery is discharged conservatively). Overridden when dynamic offset for Zone 2 is active.

surplus_enabled.d: Highest priority — blocks tariff charging, discharge-lock and AC charging while Zone 0 is active. Normal: SOC ≥ threshold + PV surplus. With forecast: PV > hard limit is sufficient (no SOC gate), exit blocked until forecast drops.

surplus_soc_threshold.d: Zone 0 only starts at this SOC. Recommended: > Zone-1 threshold. Typical: 90–98 %.

surplus_soc_hyst.d: Exit only when SOC < (threshold − hysteresis). Prevents flickering near the threshold. Typical: 3–5 %.

surplus_pv_hyst.d: Entry: PV > output + grid + hysteresis. Exit: PV ≤ output + grid − hysteresis.

surplus_forecast_enabled.d: When forecast ≥ threshold: surplus starts as soon as PV > hard limit (no SOC gate). Exit completely blocked — only Zone 3 (safety) ends surplus. In the evening the forecast sensor drops below the threshold → normal exit logic applies immediately.

surplus_forecast_sensor.d: Sensor with the predicted PV yield (e.g. Forecast.Solar, Solcast). Unit must match the threshold (e.g. kWh).

surplus_forecast_threshold.d: PV > hard limit + forecast ≥ threshold → surplus starts without SOC condition and stays active until forecast drops below threshold.

ac_enabled.d: Entry (case G): SOC < target AND no surplus active AND no AC/tariff charge AND mode ≠ '3' AND (grid + output) < −hysteresis. Abort (case H): mode = '3' AND ac_charge_active AND no tariff charge AND (SOC ≥ target OR (grid ≥ offset + hysteresis AND output = 0 W)). SOC protection (Zone 3) remains fully active. Does not start when surplus feed-in (Zone 0) or tariff charging is active.

ac_soc_target.d: Charging stops when SOC reaches this value. Recommended: > Zone-1 threshold — so Zone 1 takes over directly after charging and runs zero-export.

ac_power_limit.d: Absolute ceiling for AC charge power. Passed as max_power to the PI controller.

ac_hysteresis.d: Entry: (grid + output) < −hysteresis. Exit: grid ≥ (offset + hysteresis) AND output = 0 W. The output = 0 W guard prevents false triggering while the PI is still controlling.

ac_offset.d: Control target for grid power in AC charge mode. Negative = export targeted → PI increases charge power. Overridden when dynamic offset for Zone AC is active.

ac_p_factor.d: Proportional gain in AC charge mode. Due to the Solakon ONE hardware ramp (~25 s from min to max), keep low (~0.3–0.5). Starting value: 0.3.

ac_i_factor.d: Integral gain in AC charge mode. Starting value: 0 — only increase once P alone leaves a steady-state error. Typical: 0.05–0.1. The I-part does the actual control work.

tariff_enabled.d: Cheap (< cheap threshold): tariff charging. Medium (in between): discharge-lock — Zone 1 and Zone 2 blocked. Expensive (≥ expensive threshold): normal SOC logic. Blocked while surplus feed-in (Zone 0) is active. Independent of the AC charging module.

tariff_price_sensor.d: Sensor entity with the current electricity price as a numeric value in ct/kWh.

tariff_cheap_threshold.d: Tariff charging starts when price is below this threshold AND SOC < target. Also the lower bound of the discharge-lock.

tariff_cheap_entity.d: Optional input_number entity. When set and available, overrides the static value.

tariff_exp_threshold.d: Above this threshold: normal SOC logic, discharge-lock lifts. In between (cheap ≤ price < expensive): discharge-lock Zone 1 and Zone 2. Must be greater than the cheap threshold.

tariff_exp_entity.d: Optional input_number entity. When set and available, overrides the static value.

tariff_soc_target.d: Tariff charging stops when this SOC is reached. Independent of the AC charging module's SOC target.

tariff_power.d: Fixed charge power during tariff charging — no PI controller, no dynamic adjustment.

pv_forecast_enabled.d: When active and the forecast sensor reports a value ≥ threshold, tariff charging and discharge-lock are suppressed. Automatic flexibility for sunny days.

pv_forecast_sensor.d: input_number or sensor entity with a kWh value (e.g. expected solar generation today). Can come from a weather integration or a Solcast account.

pv_forecast_threshold.d: When forecast ≥ this value: tariff charging and discharge-lock are blocked, normal zero-export continues.

stddev_window.d: Time window for the standard deviation calculation. Applies to all zones.

dyn_z1_enabled.d: Calculates the offset dynamically from the grid StdDev instead of the static value. Overrides the static Zone-1 offset.

dyn_z1_min.d: Base buffer with a calm grid (StdDev ≤ noise threshold). Offset never falls below this value.

dyn_z1_max.d: Upper ceiling of the offset. Offset never exceeds this value, even at very high StdDev.

dyn_z1_noise.d: StdDev below this threshold is treated as measurement noise and does not trigger an offset increase.

dyn_z1_factor.d: Gain above the noise threshold. Formula: buffer = (StdDev − noise) × factor.

dyn_z1_negative.d: Negates the calculated offset (× −1). The control target then lies below 0 W.

dyn_z2_enabled.d: Calculates the offset dynamically from the grid StdDev instead of the static value. Overrides the static Zone-2 offset.

dyn_z2_min.d: Base buffer with a calm grid (StdDev ≤ noise threshold). Offset never falls below this value.

dyn_z2_max.d: Upper ceiling of the offset. Offset never exceeds this value, even at very high StdDev.

dyn_z2_noise.d: StdDev below this threshold is treated as measurement noise and does not trigger an offset increase.

dyn_z2_factor.d: Gain above the noise threshold. Formula: buffer = (StdDev − noise) × factor.

dyn_z2_negative.d: Negates the calculated offset (× −1). The control target then lies below 0 W.

dyn_ac_enabled.d: Calculates the AC charge offset dynamically from the grid StdDev instead of the static value. Overrides the static AC offset.

dyn_ac_min.d: Base buffer with a calm grid (StdDev ≤ noise threshold). Offset never falls below this value.

dyn_ac_max.d: Upper ceiling of the offset. Offset never exceeds this value, even at very high StdDev.

dyn_ac_noise.d: StdDev below this threshold is treated as measurement noise and does not trigger an offset increase.

dyn_ac_factor.d: Gain above the noise threshold. Formula: buffer = (StdDev − noise) × factor.

dyn_ac_negative.d: Negates the calculated offset (× −1). The control target then lies below 0 W.

night_enabled.d: Disable Zone 2 when PV < PV reserve. Zone 1 and AC charging continue running. Zone 2 is not reactivated while a tariff lock (medium/cheap price) is active.

top_card.title: ⚡ Solakon ONE Zero Export
top_card.p1: The Solakon ONE Zero Export integration automatically controls the inverter output power so that grid import is kept as close to 0 W as possible — without exporting to the public grid.
top_card.p2: At its core is a PI controller with grid power as the controlled variable and inverter output power as the manipulated variable. Behaviour is governed by four SOC zones: Zone 1 discharges aggressively, Zone 2 conservatively, Zone 3 blocks discharge, Zone 0 actively exports surplus.
top_card.p3: Optional modules: ☀️ Surplus, ⚡ AC Charging, 💹 Tariff Arbitrage, 📈 Dynamic Offset (per zone individually), 🌙 Night Shutdown. All parameters are stored persistently here in the panel — no YAML, no helper entities.
top_card.prio_heading: Priority and mutual blocking of optional modules:
top_card.prio_note: AC charging and tariff charging mutually block each other via the mode guard (mode ≠ '3'). Surplus feed-in has absolute priority — no other optional module can start while Zone 0 is active.

debug.pi_desc: Manually resets the I-part of the PI controller to 0. Useful after a manual intervention or strong integral windup.
debug.zone_desc: Manually switches the discharge cycle and resets the integral.

dist.single_instance_note: Power distribution is only needed with multiple instances.
dist.global_max_desc: Absolute ceiling for all instances combined — split according to weights. Default: 800 W (legal feed-in maximum). Ignored in manual mode.
dist.interval_desc: How often the distribution is recalculated and written to the coordinators.
dist.mode_desc:
Equal → each active instance receives the same share
Weighted → split by SOC ratio and/or PV power of the instance
Manual → fixed wattage per instance, independent of SOC or PV
dist.balance_desc: 0.0 → exclusively by SOC · 1.0 → exclusively by instance PV power · 0.5 → 50/50 — Only effective when mode = Weighted
